ogTitle: Burns Beach vs Burracoppin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burns Beach and Burracoppin.
heading: Burns Beach vs Burracoppin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burns Beach, Western Australia and Burracoppin, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burracoppin has a lower overall climate risk score (35/100) compared to Burns Beach (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burns Beach or Burracoppin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burns Beach has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Burracoppin scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burns Beach scores 51/100 for bushfire risk. Burracoppin scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burns Beach or Burracoppin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burracoppin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.37% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
